message:
Make explicit_links configurable in config.yaml

Add explicit_links as a configuration option so projects can set a
consistent default for link rendering across all commands. Individual
command invocations can still override the config default with CLI flags.

This solves a UX issue where release create and release notes could
produce different output depending on which flags were passed, by
allowing projects to define their preferred link rendering behavior
once in configuration.

Changes:
- Add explicit_links field to Config dataclass with default False
- Parse and validate explicit_links in load_config() and load_package_config()
- Include explicit_links in dump_config() output when enabled
- Update --explicit-links to paired flag --explicit-links/--no-explicit-links
- Resolve CLI flag against config default in release_create, release_notes, show

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+---
+title: explicit_links configuration option
+type: change
+authors:
+  - mavam
+  - claude
+created: 2025-12-24T08:00:20.139184Z
+---
+
+The `explicit_links` option can now be configured in `config.yaml`, allowing consistent link formatting across all commands that render release notes and changelog entries.
+
+Previously, the `--explicit-links` flag only worked with the `release create` command and had no persistent setting. Now you can set `explicit_links: true` in your project's `config.yaml` to enable explicit Markdown link rendering by default for all commands (`release create`, `release notes`, and `show`). Individual command invocations can still override the config default with `--explicit-links` or `--no-explicit-links` flags.
+
+This ensures consistent output across commands without requiring repeated flag usage, improving the user experience for projects that always want explicit link rendering.

 def explicit_links_option() -> Callable[[F], F]:
-    """Shared --explicit-links option for Markdown rendering commands.
+    """Shared --explicit-links/--no-explicit-links option for Markdown rendering.
 
     When enabled, converts GitHub shorthand (@mentions and #PR references)
     to explicit Markdown links. Examples:
@@ -279,6 +279,8 @@ def explicit_links_option() -> Callable[[F], F]:
     Useful for exporting release notes to documentation sites or other
     Markdown renderers that don't automatically link GitHub references.
 
+    When neither flag is specified, uses the config.explicit_links setting.
+
     Used by: release create, release notes, show
 
     IMPORTANT: Keep this decorator in sync with all commands that render

     run_show_entries(
         ctx,
         identifiers=identifiers,
